title: Hard things i have done — empty-body title-only manifesto seed
html:
TL;DR

  • Title-only Apple Note from Mar 3, 2026 (modified Mar 8) titled "Hard things i have done" — empty body.
  • Pure empty-body sub-pattern: the title is the prompt, the body never got filled, the modified-date tells us Yahir came back five days later and still didn't write anything.
  • The title is itself a manifesto style self-prompt — the same shape as "WHY?" from July 2022 and "Are you willing to sinker further and go deeper? Are you ferociously obsessed?" from Dec 2025.
  • Connects directly to the voluntary adversity synthesis — Yahir keeps trying to inventory his hard-thing résumé but rarely lands the actual list (compare with March 24 "things i've tried" which DID get filled).
  • The 5-day gap between created (Mar 3) and modified (Mar 8) suggests Yahir opened the note twice — the un-writing is itself the data.

Why it matters

The unfilled hard-things-list is one of the most revealing artifacts in the : Yahir has the prompt, has the conviction the list matters, can't bring himself to enumerate. This may be inverse-bragging-anxiety (writing your own hard-things-résumé feels like asking for credit) or scope-paralysis (which threshold counts as "hard"?). Either way it's load-bearing for voluntary adversity as philosophy — the philosophy is operative even when the receipts aren't tallied. Pair with 2026 03 24 things ive tried (the 21-day-later cousin that did get filled) for a case-study in what Yahir can vs. can't write down about himself.

Open questions

  • What hard things would have made the list? (China chapter? gym shifts? Selling the gun in 2025 09 23 how im scraping by?)
  • Is the empty-body a deferral (will fill later) or a resolved-by-not-writing (the act of titling was enough)?
  • Worth tracking: how many manifesto style note empty-body title-only notes accumulate in 2026? If 5+ they merit their own concept stub.

Wikilinks
